title: internal/mkcw/embed/entrypoint_amd64.gz: rebuild with native assembler
body:
The earlier committed .gz was built via the Go fallback path (~370KB). Rebuild it using the native assembler path (~365 bytes).

<!--

that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

This solves a frequent annoyance seen after running `make` and committing to git.

comments.body:
How about this Makefile addition and maybe add that to the `validate` CI job:

```
.PHONY: lint-entrypoint
lint-entrypoint:
        @tmp=$$(mktemp) ; \
        zcat internal/mkcw/embed/entrypoint_amd64.gz > "$$tmp" ; \
        if readelf -S "$$tmp" | grep -qE '\.go\.|gopclntab'; then \
                echo "FAIL: entrypoint_amd64.gz was built using Go fallback path" ; \
                rm -f "$$tmp" ; \
                exit 1 ; \
        fi ; \
        echo "OK: entrypoint_amd64.gz was built using native assembler" ; \
        rm -f "$$tmp"
```

I could add another commit here if there's agreement.

I am still of the opinion that shipping a binary artifact in the source is wrong to begin with and likely violates all sort of distro policies with us bundling that.

And as history shows review is not catching all these modifications so considering the fact that anyone can replace this binary with content of unkown origin we are inviting problems.

I am in favour of such a "lint" step for now though long term I think shipping binaries in the source tree is always incorrect and we should try to remove that dep.
@lsm5 I don't have a strong opinion on this one way or another.  Sure.

> @lsm5 I don't have a strong opinion on this one way or another. Sure.

@nalind ack, PTAL for review and (hopefully) merge. This should fix the recurring annoyance (at least) I notice.
We need other cleanups in this Makefile, but this'll do for now.
